consistency.spell_level_and_casting: The spell is level 6, which aligns with the high-level, complex nature of setting up a delayed, triggered effect.
consistency.components: The material component (statuette worth 1,500 gp) is substantial, fitting for a 6th-level spell that provides long-duration, conditional protection/buff.
consistency.time_and_duration: A 10-minute casting time and a 10-day duration are appropriate for establishing a long-term contingency.
consistency.contingent_spell_limitations: The requirements for the contingent spell (5th level or lower, 1 action casting time, must target self) are clear and logically restrict abuse of the feature.

quality.descriptive_clarity: The description is very clear regarding setup, activation, targeting restrictions (always targets self), and termination conditions (circumstance met, casting a new Contingency, losing the material component).
quality.mechanical_soundness: The mechanic is generally sound, functioning as a delayed, auto-triggering action. The limitation on only having one active at a time prevents stacking and excessive complexity.
quality.component_linkage: Tying the effect to the material component (the statuette) provides a crucial physical link for the magic to persist, justifying why losing it ends the effect.

mechanics.trigger: Relies on a player-defined 'circumstance,' granting high utility but requiring careful phrasing.
mechanics.resource_expenditure: Costs two spell slots upon casting (one for Contingency, one for the contingent spell), representing a high initial investment for a delayed reward.
mechanics.interaction_rules: Explicitly states the contingent spell takes effect immediately when the circumstance is met, effectively acting as an automatic, instant reaction.
mechanics.self_targeting_rule: Crucially clarifies that even if the contingent spell normally targets others, it *must* target the caster when triggered by Contingency.
